title: Discriminative Model
description: A model trained to score, rank, or classify inputs into labels or preferences rather than synthesize new content.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Discriminative models include classifiers, rerankers, and reward models used in RLHF. They consume representations from shared backbones and output decisions or scalar scores. The role is orthogonal to architecture: transformers are used in both generative and discriminative setups.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Knowing a checkpoint is discriminative sets expectations for metrics (accuracy, AUROC, calibration) and for how it pairs with generative models in pipelines.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: A BERT-style encoder with a classification head for sentiment is discriminative. The same encoder body might later be fine-tuned into a generative decoder, but the discriminative training objective only predicts labels from text.

whereItAppears.title: Where It Appears
whereItAppears.body: Discriminative model appears in moderation stacks, retrieval reranking, and alignment literature next to generative model and foundation model entries.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Discriminative versus generative describes training and inference goals, not whether the network is large or small. A reward model that scores completions is discriminative even when it sits beside a generative chat model. Discriminative is also not the same as "non-foundation": many foundation checkpoints are fine-tuned into discriminative heads.

openingSummary: A discriminative model estimates p(label | input) or preference scores from inputs rather than learning a full generative distribution over outputs.
